rows.text: CAD Agent ready. Ask me to model something.
Create car model next to the house
I'll inspect the existing house.FCStd to find the house bounding box so I can place the car beside it. Running a quick read-only FreeCADCmd to get object names and the house bounding box.
I'll add a simple car (box body + four cylinders as wheels) to the existing house.FCStd, positioned to the right of the house with a 1 m gap, and save the file. Running the mutating FreeCADCmd now.

rows.text:
I inspected the house.FCStd and added a simple car model beside the house.

Summary:
- Opened: /home/code/CADAgent/house.FCStd
- House bounding box: X [-200 → 10200], Y [-200 → 8200], Z [0 → 5424.87] (mm)
- Added a Car group with parts: CarBody (box 4500×1800×1400 mm) and Wheels (4 cylinders, radius 350 mm, width 160 mm).
- Positioned the car to the +X side of the house with a 1000 mm gap. Car bounding box: X [11200 → 15700], Y [3100 → 4900], Z [0 → 1400] mm.
- Saved changes to the same file.
- Recorded key parameters and a design note in the document sidecar.

If you'd like: I can make the car more detailed (filleted body, separate axles, wheel chamfers), orient it differently, create multiple cars, or export the car to a STEP file for use elsewhere. Which would you like next?
